task_completion.description: Open the human_insulin_gene.gb file to view the sequence and annotations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A bioinformatics tool (like UGENE) or text editor is open displaying the contents, sequence, or annotations of 'human_insulin_gene.gb'.

task_completion.description: Save the 5' UTR sequence as a FASTA file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named '5_utr.fasta' is visible in the '~/UGENE_Data/results/' directory in a file manager, terminal, or open in an editor.

task_completion.description: Save the 3' UTR sequence as a FASTA file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named '3_utr.fasta' is visible in the '~/UGENE_Data/results/' directory in a file manager, terminal, or open in an editor.

task_completion.description: Document the CDS coordinates in the statistical report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'utr_analysis_report.txt' is open and explicitly lists the CDS coordinates as 60-392 (or 60..392).

task_completion.description: Document the length and GC content of the 5' UTR in the statistical report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'utr_analysis_report.txt' is open and shows the 5' UTR length as 59 bp and GC content as approximately 57.6%.

task_completion.description: Document the length and GC content of the 3' UTR in the statistical report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'utr_analysis_report.txt' is open and shows the 3' UTR length as 73 bp and GC content as approximately 65.7%.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI application (like UGENE) to inspect the sequence and calculate statistics, rather than relying solely on terminal scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with a graphical bioinformatics application, selecting regions, or viewing sequence statistics panels.

integrity.description: The agent manually created or exported the report file rather than hallucinating the final text without opening the source data.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor or word processor is shown actively editing 'utr_analysis_report.txt' with the extracted values, or a terminal command is shown echoing the specific values into the file.

integrity.description: The agent saved the files in the explicitly requested directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file paths for the FASTA files and the text report clearly show they are located in '~/UGENE_Data/results/'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input file corresponds to NCBI RefSeq NM_000207.3. The correct CDS coordinates are bases 60-392. The 5' UTR has a length of 59 bp and a GC content of 57.63%. The 3' UTR has a length of 73 bp and a GC content of 65.75%.
